title: Performance Characterization of Deep Learning Models for Breathing-based Authentication on Resource-Constrained Devices
venue: Proc. ACM Interact. Mob. Wearable Ubiquitous Technol.

method.input_representation: 96 Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) features (32 MFCC, 32 Delta MFCC, 32 Double Delta MFCC)
method.training_strategy: Adam optimizer, 1000 epochs, mini batches of size 32, early termination

evaluation_protocol: Five-fold cross-validation with 80% training, 10% validation, and 10% testing split per fold.

reported_limitations: F-scores may not be high enough for standalone primary authentication
Scalability concerns with increasing number of users

usability.usability_issues: Requires user to perform specific breathing gestures (sniff, deep breathing).

key_contribution: Comparison of deep learning (LSTM, MLP) vs shallow models (SVM, GMM) for breathing authentication on resource-constrained hardware
Evaluation of weight quantization and matrix factorization for model compression
Analysis of GPU offloading to reduce inference latency on wearables
main_strength: Deployment on actual hardware (Pixel, LG G Watch R, Raspberry Pi 3)
Detailed study of model size vs inference time vs accuracy trade-offs
insights.notes_for_review: Paper focuses heavily on the hardware efficiency and model compression of the biometric pipeline rather than the biometric security properties.
